title: Step Through a Graph Traversal
description: A step-through p5.js MicroSim where learners predict the next reachable node in a graph traversal by clicking an adjacent node, then confirm the move with the Next button.
creator: Milena Higgins
subject: Knowledge Graphs
Graph Theory
learningObjectives: Explain how graph traversal moves from node to node along edges
Predict the next reachable node before it is revealed
Identify which nodes are adjacent to a given node
accessibility: describe() accessibility text; high-contrast node states

controls.label: Next
controls.purpose: Confirm the selected candidate node as the next step

controls.label: Reset
controls.purpose: Return to the start node with an empty path

controls.label: Candidate node
controls.purpose: Select a node adjacent to the current node as the predicted next step

assumptions: Traversal only moves between adjacent nodes; revisiting a previously-visited node is allowed, matching real graph traversal.
limitations: Demonstrates undirected adjacency only (any connected node is a valid next step); does not enforce edge direction as a traversal constraint.
